briefs.title: Commercial Carriers May Now Use Approved Traction Devices Instead of Chains on BC Roads
briefs.summary: BC amended the Motor Vehicle Act Regulations to allow drivers of commercial motor vehicles to use minister-authorized traction devices in place of chains when chain-up signs are posted, subject to written carrier authorization and compliance conditions.
briefs.action_required: Apply for written ministerial authorization to use traction devices on your commercial vehicles before relying on them in chain-up zones.
